      <title>NBA Presents '3-2-1 Lottery' Anti-Tanking Reform To GMs; Owners Vote May 28</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285351/NBA-Presents-3-2-1-Lottery-Anti-Tanking-Reform-To-GMs;-Owners-Vote-May-28</link>
      <description><p>The NBA has presented a sweeping draft overhaul to its 30 general managers called the "3-2-1 lottery," a reform that would expand the draft lottery from 14 to 16 teams and introduce a relegation zone penalizing the league's three worst records, sources told ESPN. The proposal heads to an owners' vote on May 28, with the new system set to take effect beginning with the 2027 draft.</p>
<p>The framework has drawn majority support from teams following several weeks of meetings with the board of governors, competition committee and general managers. Minor adjustments remain possible, but the core structure is largely settled, according to sources.</p>
<p>Under the proposal, non-playoff teams outside the bottom three, slots four through 10, would each receive three lottery balls. The three worst teams would receive just two balls each but are guaranteed no worse than the 12th pick. The remaining 13 lottery teams could fall as low as 16th. The 9th and 10th play-in seeds receive two lottery balls each, while losers of the 7-8 play-in games receive one ball apiece.</p>
<p>Additional restrictions include a ban on <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/lottery_winners">consecutive No. 1 pick wins</a>, a prohibition on winning three straight top-five picks and an elimination of pick protections in the 12-15 range.</p>
<p>The plan includes a sunset provision expiring after the 2029 draft, aligning with the NBA's current collective bargaining agreement. The league would also gain expanded authority to reduce lottery odds or adjust draft positions as a disciplinary measure against tanking.</p>
<p>Adam Silver identified tanking as his foremost priority following a board of governors meeting in March.</p>
<p>"I do think ultimately this is a decision that needs to be made at the ownership level," Silver said in March. "It has business implications, has basketball implications, has integrity implications for the league.</p>
<p>"So, it's one that we take very seriously, and we are going to fix it. Full stop."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 28 Apr 2026 17:53:56 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285351/NBA-Presents-3-2-1-Lottery-Anti-Tanking-Reform-To-GMs;-Owners-Vote-May-28</guid>

   <item>
      <title>Consensus No. 1 2027 NBA Draft Prospect Tyran Stokes Commits To Kansas Over Kentucky</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285355/Consensus-No-1-2027-NBA-Draft-Prospect-Tyran-Stokes-Commits-To-Kansas-Over-Kentucky</link>
      <description><p>Tyran Stokes, the nation's top-ranked senior and consensus favorite to be the No. 1 pick in the 2027 NBA Draft, has committed to Kansas, he announced Tuesday. The 6-foot-7 small forward from Rainier Beach High School in Seattle chose the Jayhawks over Kentucky following an intense months-long recruiting battle.</p>
<p>The commitment gives Bill Self a potential No. 1 overall pick for the second straight season, following Darryn Peterson's year in Lawrence. Kansas now holds the No. 2-ranked recruiting class in the country, with Stokes joining five-star point guard Taylen Kinney in the 2026 class.</p>
<p>Kentucky appeared to be the clear frontrunner entering the fall, but Kansas surged as the college season progressed. A late official visit to Lexington allowed the Wildcats to close the gap before the Jayhawks ultimately held firm against Mark Pope's program.</p>
<p>Stokes, originally from Louisville, led Rainier Beach to a state championship this past season, including a 63-point performance on senior night with assistant coaches from both Kansas and Kentucky in attendance.</p>
<p>The forward possesses a rare combination of scoring, rebounding and playmaking. On the Nike EYBL circuit last summer, he averaged 22.4 points, 10.2 rebounds and 3.7 assists while shooting 51% from the field and drawing 10.4 free throw attempts per game. His playmaking approach draws comparisons to Portland Trail Blazers forward Deni Avdija.</p>
<p>Stokes has earned three gold medals with USA Basketball, most recently at last summer's FIBA U19 Basketball World Cup. He has been regarded as the top player in his class for the better part of two years, collecting power-conference scholarship offers before his freshman season at Prolific Prep in California had even started.</p>
<p>Self enters next season with his most decorated recruiting class in years, bolstered further by transfer portal additions Leroy Blyden Jr., Keanu Dawes and Christian Reeves.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 28 Apr 2026 20:42:43 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285355/Consensus-No-1-2027-NBA-Draft-Prospect-Tyran-Stokes-Commits-To-Kansas-Over-Kentucky</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Logo_Kansas_1.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Luka Doncic Unlikely To Play In Second Round If Lakers Advance</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285357/Luka-Doncic-Unlikely-To-Play-In-Second-Round-If-Lakers-Advance</link>
      <description><p>Luka Doncic is not expected to be available for the Los Angeles Lakers' second round series against the Oklahoma City Thunder should they advance by defeating the Houston Rockets. Doncic has been out since April 2nd with a hamstring injury.</p>
<p>"My understanding is it is a slow path on the recovery path," said Shams Charania on ESPN on Tuesday. </p>
<p>Doncic still doesn't have a timetable for when he could return.</p>
<p>Doncic averaged 33.5 points, 7.7 rebounds and 8.3 assists in 35.8 minutes across 64 games in the regular season in what will likely be his sixth All-NBA First Team selection.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Brad Stevens Wins NBA Executive Of The Year For Second Time</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285346/Brad-Stevens-Wins-NBA-Executive-Of-The-Year-For-Second-Time</link>
      <description><p>Boston Celtics President of Basketball Operations Brad Stevens has been named the 2025-26 NBA Basketball Executive of the Year. Stevens claimed the award for the second time, having previously won it in 2023-24.</p>
<p>Stevens earned 69 total points in voting, collecting 11 first-place votes, four second-place votes and two third-place votes. Saleh Onsi of the Atlanta Hawks finished second with 41 points, followed by Trajan Langdon of the Detroit Pistons at 40 and Jeff Peterson of the Charlotte Hornets at 37.</p>
<p>The Celtics finished 56-26 this regular season, the second-best record in the Eastern Conference. Boston secured a top-two playoff seed for the fifth consecutive year, matching Stevens' five full seasons in his current front office role.</p>
<p>Stevens becomes the 12th executive to win the award more than once since it was first presented in 1972-73. His roster construction guided Boston through a competitive Eastern Conference without sacrificing depth or continuity.</p>
<p>Before moving into the front office, Stevens spent eight seasons as Boston's head coach. Across his 13-year tenure with the franchise in both roles, the Celtics have reached the playoffs 12 times.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 28 Apr 2026 13:27:48 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285346/Brad-Stevens-Wins-NBA-Executive-Of-The-Year-For-Second-Time</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Stevens_Brad_bos_220923.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Kevin Durant Not Expected To Play In Game 5 Due To Ankle Injury</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285349/Kevin-Durant-Not-Expected-To-Play-In-Game-5-Due-To-Ankle-Injury</link>
      <description><p>Kevin Durant is expected to miss Game 5 of the Houston Rockets' first round series at the Los Angeles Lakers.</p>
<p>Durant has missed the past two games due to a sprained left ankle and bone bruise. Durant also missed Game 1 of the series due to a knee injury he picked up during practice leading to the series.</p>
<p>Durant scored 23 points in Game 2, which was also when he suffered the ankle injury.</p>
<p>The Rockets enter Game 5 trailing the Lakers 3-1 in the series. The Rockets were already without Fred VanVleet and Steven Adams, while the Lakers have played the entire series without Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves. However, Reaves is expected to return for Game 5.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 28 Apr 2026 15:05:10 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285349/Kevin-Durant-Not-Expected-To-Play-In-Game-5-Due-To-Ankle-Injury</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Durant_Kevin_hou_251010.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Steve Kerr's Future With Warriors Could Come Down To Negotiating More Than Sentimentality</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285350/Steve-Kerrs-Future-With-Warriors-Could-Come-Down-To-Negotiating-More-Than-Sentimentality</link>
      <description><p>Steve Kerr and the Golden State Warriors are expected to soon meet to discuss whether he will continue his tenure as head coach. The situation is described as being far more complicated than whether or not Kerr wants to continue coaching as there is a financial element to it and also questions of whether he will be required to make coaching changes in terms of both his staff and his style.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">"I think Steve Kerr has sent the message out that he would be very interested in returning to coach the Warriors," said Brian Windhorst on Yahoo Sports Daily. "But what I think people have misunderstood about this [is that] this is not a straight emotional decision, like, 'Do I have it within me to coach the Warriors or not?'</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">"There's not a contract sitting on the table. It's not like the Warriors said, 'Hey man, sign this at any time, whenever you feel like it.' They did not have contract extension discussions. They called those off. Steve called those off.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">"He is making the highest salary amongst all NBA coaches at $17 million, and the team has not performed at, you know, that's a championship-competition-level salary. This team is not a championship contender right now.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">"The other thing, and my colleagues Ramona Shelburne and Anthony Slater have reported on this, there is some desire within the organization for Steve Kerr to potentially change his coaching staff, and some desire for Steve Kerr to change some strategy, particularly offensively.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">"Now, that is not a controversial statement. When a team that is this expensive and this veteran underachieves and misses the playoffs, to say that you want potential changes is not a shocking thing.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">"But the question is: is Kerr willing to make those changes? Is Kerr willing to coach for a different salary next year? These are the real questions. This is where the rubber meets the road. He obviously has to ask himself the question of whether he can invest himself in the job, but he's indicated that he'd like to.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">"This might come down to a negotiating thing. It might not be sentimental at all. This might be good old-fashioned negotiating. Steve Kerr can leave the sideline and walk right into a broadcasting job, in a selection of places, for big money and an easier life. So his leverage is there that he has that out there.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">"This is not the framing that I think people have in mind that it's just, you know, does he wipe away the tears and sign the contract? There are other things to be dealt with here."</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal leading-[1.7]">The Warriors hired Kerr away from his broadcasting role with TNT in 2014 to replace Mark Jackson and they went on to reach five straight Finals and win three championships with a fourth title coming in 2022. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Golden-State-Warriors/9/Playoff-History">The Warriors have only won two playoff series over the past four seasons</a>. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 28 Apr 2026 15:36:16 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285350/Steve-Kerrs-Future-With-Warriors-Could-Come-Down-To-Negotiating-More-Than-Sentimentality</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Kerr_Steve_gsw_240113.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Austin Reaves Optimistic About Returning For Game 5</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285340/Austin-Reaves-Optimistic-About-Returning-For-Game-5</link>
      <description><p>Los Angeles Lakers guard Austin Reaves is optimistic about returning for Game 5 of his team's first-round playoff series against the Houston Rockets. The Lakers lead 3-1 and have a chance to close out the series at home in Los Angeles on Wednesday night.</p>
<p>Reaves was reportedly close to playing in Game 4, but with a 3-0 series lead, the Lakers gave their starting guard an extra couple of days. The pending unrestricted free agent has missed approximately four weeks due to oblique strain. Reaves made considerable progress with the injury and was questionable to play in Sunday's Game 4.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Franz Wagner Diagnosed With Right Calf Strain, Status For Game 5 Uncertain</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285348/Franz-Wagner-Diagnosed-With-Right-Calf-Strain-Status-For-Game-5-Uncertain</link>
      <description><p>Franz Wagner has been diagnosed with a right calf strain and his status for Game 5 in uncertain. The Orlando Magic hold a 3-1 series lead over the Detroit Pistons following their Game 4 win.</p>
<p>Wagner exited Game 4 with the injury and underwent an MRI on Tuesday.</p>
<p>The Magic will make a determination on Wagner's availability depending on how he responds to treatment.</p>
<p>Wagner averaged 20.6 points, 5.2 rebounds and 3.3 assists in 30.0 minutes across 34 games this season with the Magic.</p>
<p>Jamal Cain gave the Magic a major spark in Game 4 and figures to have his minutes increased again if Wagner is unavailable to play.</p></description>

   </item>
   <item>
      <title>AJ Dybantsa Considered Most Probable No. 1 Pick Ahead Of 2026 NBA Draft Lottery</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285344/AJ-Dybantsa-Considered-Most-Probable-No-1-Pick-Ahead-Of-2026-NBA-Draft-Lottery</link>
      <description><p>BYU freshman AJ Dybantsa has risen to the top of the 2026 NBA Draft board, with league executives broadly viewing him as the likely No. 1 overall selection. </p>
<p>Dybantsa averaged 25.5 points, 6.8 rebounds and 3.7 assists across 35 games this season. NBA scouts cite his elite physical tools, scoring potential and defensive upside as key factors driving his ascent. No true consensus exists, however, and the final decision will rest with <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/lottery_simulator">whichever team wins the lottery</a>.</p>
<p>The Washington Wizards hold the strongest lottery positioning and are projected to select Dybantsa first overall. With Trae Young and Anthony Davis already on the roster, the Wizards appear to be pivoting toward contention in the Eastern Conference.</p>
<p>This year's early-entry pool is notably lean, with just 71 players declaring, the fewest in over two decades. The rise of NIL funding has allowed college programs to retain talent, thinning draft depth outside the lottery. Despite this, the top of the board remains exceptionally strong.</p>
<p>Kansas freshman Darryn Peterson, Duke's Cameron Boozer and North Carolina's Caleb Wilson round out the projected top four. Indiana, Brooklyn and Utah hold the corresponding lottery slots.</p>

   <item>
      <title>Darryn Peterson's Work Ethic Not In Question As NBA Teams Focus On Health</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285345/Darryn-Petersons-Work-Ethic-Not-In-Question-As-NBA-Teams-Focus-On-Health</link>
      <description><p>Front office sources across the NBA have offered largely positive assessments of Kansas guard Darryn Peterson, describing him as a dedicated worker passionate about his craft. Health, not talent or character, remains the central concern as teams evaluate his No. 1 overall candidacy ahead of the May 10 combine in Chicago.</p>
<p>Peterson averaged 20.2 points, 4.2 rebounds and 1.6 assists in 24 games this season before a preseason full-body cramping episode disrupted his year both physically and mentally. The incident limited his availability and prompted deeper scrutiny from franchises holding top lottery picks.</p>
<p>Many executives viewed Peterson as a straightforward No. 1 selection entering the fall. His elite shooting and offensive skill set remain highly regarded. Teams now want confirmation from his medical evaluation at the combine that they can count on the best version of him at the next level.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285344/AJ-Dybantsa-Considered-Most-Probable-No-1-Pick-Ahead-Of-2026-NBA-Draft-Lottery">BYU's AJ Dybantsa is currently projected as the probable top pick</a>, with Peterson slotted second to the Indiana Pacers in the latest ESPN mock. However, a clean medical report could reignite direct competition between the two for the top spot.</p>
<p>The Indiana  Pacers hold a 52.1% chance of retaining a top-four pick. If the selection falls outside that range, it transfers to the Los Angeles Clippers. A healthy Peterson paired with a returning Tyrese Haliburton could quickly push Indiana back into Eastern Conference playoff contention.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 28 Apr 2026 11:47:41 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285345/Darryn-Petersons-Work-Ethic-Not-In-Question-As-NBA-Teams-Focus-On-Health</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Peterson_Darryn_ku_251105.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NCAA Tournament Expansion To 76 Teams On Track For 2026-27</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285356/NCAA-Tournament-Expansion-To-76-Teams-On-Track-For-2026-27</link>
      <description><p>The NCAA has launched the final steps toward expanding both the men's and women's basketball tournaments from 68 to 76 teams, sources told ESPN. The move is expected to be formalized within weeks and would take effect for the upcoming 2026-27 season, with a mid-May announcement possible.</p>
<p>Remaining committee approvals are considered procedural at this stage. "They have what they need to move forward," one source said.</p>
<p>NCAA officials met with men's tournament media partners last week to finalize contract terms, though agreements had not yet been signed as of Tuesday. Committee approvals, spanning the men's and women's basketball committees, oversight committees, the Division I cabinet and board of governors, require signed contracts before formal votes can occur.</p>
<p>The NCAA cautioned in a statement that "no final recommendations or decisions have been made at this time."</p>
<p>Power conferences have been the primary force behind the expansion, seeking additional at-large berths as their membership has grown throughout the current media rights cycle. Financial gain is a secondary factor, with sources describing the expected revenue increase as delivering only "a modest financial upside" after covering added logistics costs and additional NCAA tournament units.</p>
<p>Under the proposed men's tournament structure, eight additional at-large bids would be introduced. The current First Four format, four games involving eight teams in Dayton, Ohio, would grow to 12 games across 24 teams at two sites. Dayton is expected to retain one of those locations, with a second site west of the Eastern time zone still to be determined.</p>
<p>The expanded opening round would feature 24 teams competing on the Tuesday and Wednesday before the traditional 64-team bracket tips off Thursday, largely unchanged in structure, though more at-large qualifiers would be required to play in the preliminary round.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 28 Apr 2026 21:54:13 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285356/NCAA-Tournament-Expansion-To-76-Teams-On-Track-For-2026-27</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/logo_ncaa.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Nuggets Force Game 6 Behind Jokic's Triple-Double; McDaniels: 'We Going To Win The Next One'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285342/Nuggets-Force-Game-6-Behind-Jokics-Triple-Double;-McDaniels-We-Going-To-Win-The-Next-One</link>
      <description><p>Nikola Jokic delivered a 27-point, 16-assist, 12-rebound triple-double Monday to keep the Denver Nuggets' season alive, as they defeated the Minnesota Timberwolves 125-113 in Game 5. The series shifts to Minneapolis for Game 6 on Thursday with <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">the Nuggets favored by 6.5 points</a>. Oddsmakers now give the Nuggets a 61 percent probability of winning the series and completing the 3-1 comeback.</p>
<p>Denver entered as an 11-point favorite against a Timberwolves squad already missing injured stars Anthony Edwards and Donte DiVincenzo. Despite the favorable matchup, the Nuggets had dropped three straight games in increasingly deflating fashion before Monday's rebound. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Denver-Nuggets/7/playoff-history">The Nuggets completed two 3-1 series comebacks in 2020</a> and also won three straight against the Wolves in the 2024 Western Conference semifinals before losing the final two.</p>
<p>"Just an embarrassing first four games of the series," Christian Braun said.</p>
<p>Jokic's supporting cast rose to the occasion alongside him. Spencer Jones scored 20 points on four three-pointers in place of the injured Aaron Gordon, while Cam Johnson, acquired in an offseason deal that sent Michael Porter Jr. to the Brooklyn Nets, posted 18 points. The Nuggets forced 25 turnovers and converted them into 35 points.</p>
<p>Denver also shot 38% from three-point range after a collective 28.5% mark through the first four games.</p>
<p>"Jok is Jok," said Nuggets coach David Adelman. "These [small] sample size games and the freakouts when he doesn't play well. You know he's going to play well. Then his 16 assists. Assists are hard to get unless you make shots."</p>
<p>Timberwolves forward Jaden McDaniels struggled in his most difficult outing of the series, committing four turnovers and missing all three three-point attempts after drawing early foul trouble. McDaniels had sparked a late-game skirmish in Game 4 and publicly called several Nuggets players "bad defenders" following Game 2.</p>
<p>"Nothing dirty," McDaniels said. "It's all fun to me. I feed into it. We just got to get the job done next game, and there's no more talking."</p>
<p>Gordon and Peyton Watson remain questionable for Denver ahead of Game 6.</p>
<p>"We just ended up losing today," McDaniels said. "But we going to win the next one."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 28 Apr 2026 10:58:01 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285342/Nuggets-Force-Game-6-Behind-Jokics-Triple-Double;-McDaniels-We-Going-To-Win-The-Next-One</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Jokic_Nikola_den_250131.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Devin Booker Says Thunder Have 'All The Answers To The Test' Following Sweep</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285341/Devin-Booker-Says-Thunder-Have-All-The-Answers-To-The-Test-Following-Sweep</link>
      <description><p>The Oklahoma City Thunder eliminated the Phoenix Suns in a first-round sweep Monday, winning Game 4 by a 131-122 final.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Oklahoma-City-Thunder/33/playoff-history">Oklahoma City has now won 12 consecutive first-round games</a>, sweeping the opening round in each of the past three postseasons. The Thunder advance to face either the Houston Rockets or Los Angeles Lakers in the Western Conference semifinals.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/news/nba-championship-betting-odds/">The Thunder are currently -115 favorites to win the 2026 NBA championship</a>, which puts their probability at 53.5 percent</p>
<p>Shai Gilgeous-Alexander posted 31 points and eight assists in the clincher, averaging 33.8 points and 8.0 assists across the series. Chet Holmgren added 24 points and Ajay Mitchell contributed 22, as six Thunder players finished in double figures.</p>
<p>"He's the MVP of the league, and he's playing the best basketball in the league for the past two years," Devin Booker said of Gilgeous-Alexander. "That's mixed with guys that know their roles, some seasoned vets, and they're relentless. With that chemistry, the communication, it's on 100%. And when the bench comes in, they get better sometimes. It's like reinforcement, not subs. They have all the answers to the test."</p>
<p>Phoenix finished 45-37 under rookie head coach Jordan Ott, a nine-win improvement from last season, despite widespread expectations of another lottery finish. The Suns had previously shed Kevin Durant via trade and waived Bradley Beal ahead of the rebuild.</p>
<p>"We're supposed to be a laughingstock, a losing team, from all media and whoever else said it," said Dillon Brooks. "We proved a lot of people wrong, and that's just because we had heart. We came together, we played with each other and we believed in each other. So, even though it sucks that you get swept in the playoffs by a great team, it doesn't go against our season and what we're building here."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 28 Apr 2026 10:43:48 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285341/Devin-Booker-Says-Thunder-Have-All-The-Answers-To-The-Test-Following-Sweep</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Gilgeousalexander_Shai_okc_260406F.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Dylan Mingo Commits To Baylor</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285339/Dylan-Mingo-Commits-To-Baylor</link>
      <description><p>Five-star guard Dylan Mingo committed to the Baylor Bears on Monday for the 2026 class.</p>
<p>Mingo previously committed to North Carolina, but decommitted after the school fired Hubert Davis. </p>
<p>"I believe coach [Scott] Drew was a big factor in helping VJ Edgecombe improve and prepare for the NBA," Mingo said. "He had some tough moments as a freshman and coach Drew helped him through it. They have produced a lot of professional players, especially in the backcourt."</p>
<p>Mingo will join his brother Kayden in Waco.</p>
<p>"Playing with my brother Kayden is a big plus," Mingo added. "It is a blessing to play with him again. We had success in high school with VJ Edgecombe and Nigel James. We have been playing together since we were little kids, and there is a built-in chemistry and trust you can't duplicate. We keep each other accountable, too. Baylor was one of my final schools before, so I was comfortable with them."</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Jamahl Mosley Following Magic Win To Take 3-1 Series Lead: 'Right Now It Means Nothing'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285337/Jamahl-Mosley-Following-Magic-Win-To-Take-3-1-Series-Lead-Right-Now-It-Means-Nothing</link>
      <description><p>The Orlando Magic defeated the Detroit Pistons 94-88 in Game 4 on Monday night to take a 3-1 series lead.</p>
<p>"We put our ourselves in position to try to get four, but right now it means nothing," Magic coach Jamahl Mosley said. "We have the advantage and now we have to try and make sure we keep that advantage."</p>
<p>Desmond Bane lead the way with 22 points, with Franz Wagner adding 19 points.</p>
<p>Wagner would leave the game with right calf soreness in the third quarter.</p>
<p>A No. 8 seed has beaten the No. 1 seed just four times since the playoffs expanded to best-of-seven for all rounds in 2003.</p>
<p>"This is a team that won 60 games. I'm sure they're not blinking an eye about not being able to win three games in a row," Bane said. "They did it multiple times throughout the regular season."</p>
<p>Cade Cunningham lead the Pistons with 25 points, with Tobias Harris adding 20 points.  </p>
<p>Harris added that the Pistons need to take care of the ball.</p>
<p>"We went into the series saying we needed to win a possession game," Harris said. "That came down to the rebounding battle and also taking care of the basketball and in all of our losses that's what we haven't been at our best at. Obviously, we need to limit them from going to the free-throw line. They've been able to get us in close-out situations and break us down, get to the lane."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">Game 5 is on Wednesday at Detroit with the Pistons favored by 9.5 points</a>. However, oddsmakers give the Magic a 73 percent probability of eventually winning the series. </p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Terry Rozier Faces Superseding Indictment As Prosecutors Allege He Solicited, Accepted Bribe</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285334/Terry-Rozier-Faces-Superseding-Indictment-As-Prosecutors-Allege-He-Solicited-Accepted-Bribe</link>
      <description><p>Federal prosecutors plan to file a superseding indictment against Terry Rozier, adding bribery allegations to an existing sports gambling case. A lawyer from the U.S. Attorney's Office for the Eastern District of New York announced the new charges Monday during a federal court hearing.</p>
<p>The disclosure came as Rozier's attorney, Jim Trusty, was seeking dismissal of the original case. Trusty told the court prosecutors had informed him of the new charges just two hours before the hearing. Rozier has pleaded not guilty to the initial counts.</p>
<p>"We have developed evidence that Mr. Rozier solicited and accepted a bribe," said Eastern District prosecutor Kaitlin Ferrell during Monday's hearing.</p>
<p>Prosecutors will present the charges to a grand jury, alleging sports bribery and honest services fraud. They contend Rozier deprived "the NBA and the Charlotte Hornets of Mr. Rozier's honest services." The superseding indictment is expected by mid-May.</p>
<p>Rozier currently faces two federal wire fraud charges stemming from an October arrest. Prosecutors allege he tipped off associate Deniro Laster about plans to exit a March 2023 game early while playing for Charlotte. Laster allegedly sold that information to gamblers who wagered on Rozier's playing time.</p>
<p>The Miami Heat waived Rozier earlier this month. The NBA had suspended him following the original charges, though an arbitrator ruled he must still receive his $26.4 million salary. A status hearing is scheduled for June 10.</p>
<p>Rozier is one of six defendants in a broad federal case alleging non-public NBA player information was sold to sports gamblers. A seventh defendant, former NBA player Damon Jones, is set to change his plea to guilty Tuesday, becoming the first defendant in the case to do so. Jones will also plead guilty in a separate case involving an alleged rigged poker ring, which includes former NBA player Chauncey Billups, who has pleaded not guilty.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 27 Apr 2026 22:32:46 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285334/Terry-Rozier-Faces-Superseding-Indictment-As-Prosecutors-Allege-He-Solicited-Accepted-Bribe</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Rozier_Terry_mia_240226.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA Expansion Bidding More Competitive In Las Vegas Than Seattle</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285333/NBA-Expansion-Bidding-More-Competitive-In-Las-Vegas-Than-Seattle</link>
      <description><p>Multiple investor groups have expressed early interest in NBA expansion franchises in Las Vegas and Seattle, with the league targeting fees as high as $10 billion per team. Las Vegas is drawing stronger initial interest than Seattle, according to sources familiar with the discussions.</p>
<p>No formal bids have been submitted. The league's financial adviser, PJT Partners, is screening potential ownership groups ahead of a structured bidding process expected later this year. PJT and the NBA declined to comment.</p>
<p>League executives believe each franchise could fetch between $7 billion and $10 billion. The Los Angeles Lakers sold for $10 billion last year, the only majority-stake sports transaction globally to reach that figure. The Boston Celtics set a prior benchmark in 2025 at $6.1 billion.</p>
<p>"The only discussion in the room was understanding the math around dilution, in terms of projection, and what's the direct reduction in existing revenues to teams if we were to expand beginning in 2028 or 2029," Commissioner Adam Silver said in March.</p>
<p>Among those pursuing a Las Vegas franchise are NBA Hall of Famer Magic Johnson, Vegas Golden Knights owner Bill Foley, and Avenue Capital Group founder Marc Lasry. Johnson met with Nevada Governor Joe Lombardo earlier this year regarding a potential bid.</p>
<p>Foley, who co-controls T-Mobile Arena with Anschutz Entertainment Group and MGM Resorts International, confirmed his interest to a Nevada television station in March.</p>
<p>On the Seattle side, Samantha Holloway, through her firm One Roof Sports and Entertainment, which oversees the NHL's Kraken ownership group, is preparing a bid. Seattle has been without an NBA franchise since the SuperSonics relocated to Oklahoma City in 2008.</p>
<p>"We are ready to work with the Commissioner and the league, at their direction, to explore bringing the NBA back to Seattle," a One Roof spokesperson said.</p>
<p>The NBA's board of governors is expected to vote later this year on approving one or both franchises. The league's last expansion came in 2004, when Charlotte paid $300 million for the Bobcats.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 27 Apr 2026 22:20:12 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285333/NBA-Expansion-Bidding-More-Competitive-In-Las-Vegas-Than-Seattle</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_realgm.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Cooper Flagg Beats Out Kon Knueppel To Win 2025-26 NBA Rookie Of The Year</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285332/Cooper-Flagg-Beats-Out-Kon-Knueppel-To-Win-2025-26-NBA-Rookie-Of-The-Year</link>
      <description><p>Cooper Flagg has been named the <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/awards/by-type/Rookie-of-the-Year/5">2025-26 NBA Rookie of the Year</a>, beating out former Duke teammate Kon Knueppel. Flagg received 56 first place votes compared to 44 for Knueppel  </p>
<p>Flagg averaged 21.0 points, 6.7 rebounds, 4.5 assists and 1.2 steals in 33.5 minutes over 70 games for the Dallas Mavericks. Flagg was the No. 1 overall pick in the 2025 NBA draft out of Duke. </p>
<p>"This is a truly great honor," said Flagg. "I'm grateful to receive this award and thankful to everyone in the Dallas Mavericks organization who believed in me from day one. None of this happens without my teammates, coaches and the people around me pushing me every day. I came here to compete and help this team win. This is just one step forward in what we're building." </p>
<p>Knueppel appeared in 81 games for the Charlotte Hornets, averaging 18.5 points, 5.3 rebounds and 3.4 assists. Knueppel shot 42.5 percent on 642 three-point attempts, making a rookie record 273 three-pointers.</p>
<p>VJ Edgecombe finished third in Rookie of the Year voting.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Paige Bueckers Calls Relationship With Azzi Fudd Private, Pledges Professionalism On Court</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285331/Paige-Bueckers-Calls-Relationship-With-Azzi-Fudd-Private-Pledges-Professionalism-On-Court</link>
      <description><p>Dallas Wings star Paige Bueckers used her opening statement at Monday's media day to directly address public interest in her relationship with teammate Azzi Fudd, declaring the matter private while pledging full professionalism on the court.</p>
<p>"Quite frankly, I believe me and Azzi's personal relationship is nobody's business but our own, and what we choose to share is completely up to us," said Bueckers, the reigning WNBA Rookie of the Year and the No. 1 overall pick in the 2025 draft.</p>
<p>Bueckers made clear she intends to address the topic only once. Future questions, she said, will be met with deflection or a reference back to Monday's statement.</p>
<p>"Me and Azzi have always been the utmost professional," Bueckers said. "We've never let anything that happens off the court carry onto the court. That's what we continue to do."</p>
<p>Both were top-ranked high school recruits who played together with USA Basketball before becoming UConn teammates for four years, winning a national championship together in 2025.</p>
<p>Fudd was selected No. 1 in this month's WNBA draft after a standout senior season at UConn, averaging 17.3 points and shooting 45 percent from three. Wings general manager Curt Miller said the evaluation process "always came back to Azzi" regardless of her connection to Bueckers.</p>
<p>"Azzi Fudd was a No. 1 draft pick because she earned it, and it had nothing to do with me," Bueckers said.</p>
<p>Dallas opens its season May 9 against the Indiana Fever in a matchup featuring the last four No. 1 overall picks.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Pat Riley Vows To Stay With Heat, Demands Roster Overhaul Around Bam Adebayo</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285329/Pat-Riley-Vows-To-Stay-With-Heat-Demands-Roster-Overhaul-Around-Bam-Adebayo</link>
      <description><p>Miami Heat president Pat Riley declared Monday he has no plans to leave the organization while expressing sharp frustration over the franchise's prolonged struggles, speaking at his annual end-of-season press conference.</p>
<p>"I'm not going to retire. I'm not going to resign. I'm not going to step aside," Riley said. "I want another parade down Biscayne Boulevard."</p>
<p>Miami finished 43-39, tenth in the Eastern Conference, marking the club's fourth straight Play-In Tournament appearance. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/team-stats/2025/Advanced_Stats/Team_Totals/Regular_Season/ediff/desc">The Heat ranked 12th in offensive efficiency and 14th in defense</a>, their worst defensive standing relative to the league since 2014-15.</p>
<p>Riley singled out Bam Adebayo, who turns 29 in July, as the foundation of any rebuild. Adebayo averaged 20 points and 10 rebounds for the first time in his career but shot a career-low 44.2 percent from the field while expanding his perimeter game. Riley called efficiency his primary concern going forward.</p>
<p>"He deserves to win more," Riley said of Adebayo, ruling out any possibility of trading him.</p>
<p>The Heat also navigated a difficult season that included Tyler Herro appearing in just 33 games, Nikola Jovic shooting a career-low 36.6 percent, and the waiving of Terry Rozier following a federal sports gambling indictment.</p>
<p>Bright spots included Norman Powell's first All-Star selection and Bam Adebayo's 83-point performance against Washington on March 10, the second-highest single-game scoring total in NBA history.</p>
<p>"We're not good enough, and we're not happy with it," Riley said. "I think this is the first time in three years we can do something about it."</p></description>

      <title>Five-Star Finnish Forward Miikka Muurinen Commits To Arkansas Vaulting To No. 1 Recruiting Class</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285328/Five-Star-Finnish-Forward-Miikka-Muurinen-Commits-To-Arkansas-Vaulting-To-No-1-Recruiting-Class</link>
      <description><p>Finland native Miikka Muurinen, a five-star forward with a 95 scouting grade, has committed to Arkansas, giving John Calipari the No. 1 recruiting class in the country, the 7-footer told ESPN Monday.</p>
<p>Muurinen spent time at several American high schools before signing with Partizan Belgrade in Serbia, where he appeared in 14 games this season including six in EuroLeague play. He took an official visit to Arkansas in fall 2024 and maintained close contact with the program in the 18 months since.</p>
<p>"Since my visit a year and a half ago, they've been consistent and committed to recruiting me," Muurinen told ESPN. "When I left my Arkansas visit, I was not only excited about the program but also about the people, and I said to myself, those are the type of people I want to be around on a daily basis."</p>
<p>"The thing about Coach Calipari that stood out was that he kept it real with me," Muurinen said. "He has had so much success with so many one-and-done players, and I really like his coaching style both on and off the court."</p>
<p>Muurinen joins a loaded 2026 class that includes top-ranked guard Jordan Smith Jr., five-star forward JaShawn Andrews, and top-30 wing Abdou Toure. The addition gives Arkansas four likely five-star prospects and a projected preseason top-10 roster.</p>
<p>At the Nike Hoop Summit earlier this month, Muurinen posted 10 points and eight rebounds in 15 minutes for the World Team. He also represented Finland's senior national team at FIBA EuroBasket last summer, averaging 6.6 points in eight games.</p>
<p>"I'm going to Arkansas to help them win a national championship," Muurinen said, "and to play with a great roster of guys."</p></description>

      <title>Jayson Tatum's Achilles Recovery Aided By Surgical Advances Honed On Ballerinas</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285322/Jayson-Tatums-Achilles-Recovery-Aided-By-Surgical-Advances-Honed-On-Ballerinas</link>
      <description><p>Jayson Tatum's rapid recovery from a torn Achilles was aided by his proximity to one of the world's best surgeons who had performed the procedure for years on elite ballerinas. </p>
<p>Tatum ruptured his Achilles during a playoff game at Madison Square Garden last May. Within 16 hours, he was on the operating table at the Hospital for Special Surgery in Manhattan, under the care of surgeon Martin O'Malley. Before treating NBA stars, O'Malley learned his craft from ballerinas.</p>
<p>Long before repairing the Achilles tendons of players such as Kevin Durant, the first All-NBA player in recent history to return to peak form after rupturing the ligament, O'Malley trained under the primary surgeon for the American Ballet Theatre. His earliest prominent patients were New York's top ballerinas. Operating on those performers taught him a technique that leaves the repaired tendon slightly tighter than the healthy one, preventing rehabilitation from stretching it loose.</p>
<p>"Those are not easy ones," O'Malley said. "To get them where they can walk on their toes for six months."</p>
<p>After years studying the 10-inch tendon that can end athletic careers, O'Malley arrived at the guiding principle that the sooner a patient reaches the operating table, the stronger their chances of full recovery.</p>
<p>O'Malley's clinic is located less than two miles from Madison Square Garden, allowing for an unusually rapid surgical response following Tatum's injury.</p>
<p>"As soon as the Achilles ruptures, things are going the wrong way," O'Malley said. "You get atrophy, change in physiology from the minute you rupture it. The best thing to do is to fix it faster."</p>
<p>The Celtics, anticipating a full season lost, restructured significant portions of their roster following Tatum's injury. Instead, he returned in March and has since played at an All-Star level.</p>
<p>"I had a motivated, freak athlete," O'Malley said. "So that really helped me a lot."</p>
<p>"A lot of things had to go right," Tatum said, "to even have the opportunity to come back and play."</p></description>

   </item>

      <title>Kevin Durant Not Ruled Out Of Returning In Lakers' Series</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285321/Kevin-Durant-Not-Ruled-Out-Of-Returning-In-Lakers-Series</link>
      <description><p>Kevin Durant remains sidelined with an ankle bone bruise, but Houston Rockets coach Ime Udoka left the door open for the veteran forward's return after Sunday's 115-96 Game 4 victory over the Los Angeles Lakers extended the first-round playoff series.</p>
<p>Durant has now missed three of four games this postseason. He sat out the series opener with a bruised right knee, returned for Game 2, scoring 23 points in 41 minutes before re-injuring his ankle late, then missed Games 3 and 4.</p>
<p>"Yeah, a possibility, for sure," Udoka said. "I didn't know how bad it was initially and then we got that prognosis. But he's doing what he can to get swelling out and mobility back. And just like the knee, we weren't sure when he was going to come back, but he snapped back pretty quickly to be available for Game 2. So it is a true game-to-game, day-to-day thing."</p>
<p>Udoka described the bone bruise as painful and restricting Durant's movement, while stopping short of ruling him out for the remainder of the playoffs. Game 5 tips off Wednesday night in Los Angeles.</p>
<p>Houston dropped Game 3 Friday without Durant, surrendering a six-point lead in the final 30 seconds of regulation before falling 112-108 in overtime. The Rockets responded Sunday behind strong contributions from healthy roster members to keep their postseason run alive.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 27 Apr 2026 11:13:58 EDT</pubDate>
